title: lora_recv buffer bound differs from callback buffer
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:160-161,170

rx_thread declares rx_buf[LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD] and passes sizeof(rx_buf) to lora_recv(). The callback then passes rx_buf with ret (actual length) to the callback.

BUILD_ASSERT at line 161-162 verifies sizeof(rx_buf) == LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD.

The code is correct but the callback signature (line 225) passes ret as size_t. If ret somehow became negative after the > 0 check (race, memory corruption), it would wrap to a huge positive value when cast to size_t in the callback.

Extremely unlikely given the flow, but the callback could do its own bounds check against LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD.
close_reason: Enhanced BUILD_ASSERT message and added block comment documenting that rx_buf is sized to LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD (255 bytes) and callback callers must be prepared to handle up to this size
